description: Visual arts and how it relates to individuals, their community, and the importance in a cultural and global context. Understand vocabulary of the visual arts, and develop strategies for observation and critical thinking through assignments including reflective writing and observational analysis on various works of art. Engage with various visual art topics and disciplines such as: defining what art is, learning about types of art and its use and meaning of materials, how art plays a role in the expression of humanity, and how personal awareness and perspective can benefit from the engagement of art. Meets International Perspectives Requirement.

Exploration of the nature of visual perception in relation to issues of visual communication, problem solving, envisioning information, and visual thinking. Studio assignments to be digitized and sent to instructor electronically for evaluation and critique.
Half-semester course. Introduction to wood's physical properties, its potential as an expressive medium, and basic woodworking tools and techniques.
Half semester course. Introduction to basic jewelry/metals design and fabrication. Forming, texturing, and joining techniques (soldering/riveting) will be explored and applied to two projects.

Half-semester course introducing ceramic techniques including hand-building, high fire and low fire glaze applications and expressive approaches that will be applied to two projects. The emphasis is on creative communication through ceramics.

prerequisites: Sophomore classification
description: Introduction to relief, monoprint, intaglio, lithography, and screen printing as methods for visual communication and expression.

prerequisites: Sophomore classification
description: Half-semester course. Introduction to color theory and color systems using various media for visual communication and creative expression.

prerequisites: Sophomore classification
description: Introduction to film camera operation and traditional black and white darkroom methods for visual communication and creative expression. Film cameras required for class but may be checked out for short periods during semester.

prerequisites: Sophomore classification
description: Introduction to digital media tools and concepts and digital fabrication processes to create two dimensional, three dimensional, and time-based artworks. Students will be introduced to major digital art and design software packages.

prerequisites: Sophomore classification
description: Half-semester course. Introduction to preparation of painting grounds, color mixing, manipulation of paint and pictorial space as methods for visual communication and expression.

prerequisites: Sophomore classification
description: Half semester course. Introduction to two-dimensional and three-dimensional textile techniques used for visual communication and expression.

The functions and operations of the digital camera, scanning and other image input devices, digital image manipulation, software usage and support, color management and printing, presentation of images, compositional dynamics and the development of 'seeing' as a medium of design, expression, and communication. Students should have access to a good or high quality digital camera with the ability to separately adjust shutter speed, f/stop and exposure, a laptop with updated Adobe Photoshop software, and enough digital storage for all class assignments.

title_name: Introduction to Creative Digital Photography: Honors
prerequisites: Membership in the University Honors Program
description: The functions and operations of the digital camera, scanning and other image input devices, digital image manipulation, software usage and support, color management and printing, presentation of images, compositional dynamics and the development of 'seeing' as a medium of design, expression, and communication. Students should have access to a good or high quality digital camera with the ability to separately adjust shutter speed, f/stop and exposure, a laptop with updated Adobe Photoshop software, and enough digital storage for all class assignments.

prerequisites: DSNS 1020 , DSNS 1310 , and DSNS 1830
description: A continuation of DSNS 1310 . Further development of perceptual drawing skills from a variety of subject matter. Continued practice with drawing materials and techniques with emphasis on tonal and color media.

Fundamentals of painting using water-based media applied to observation-based painting. Subject matter may include working from actual or two-dimensional references of still life, landscape, architectural space, and the human form.

prerequisites: ART 2300 ; Membership in the University Honors Program
description: Fundamentals of painting using water-based media applied to observation-based painting. Subject matter may include working from actual or two-dimensional references of still life, landscape, architectural space, and the human form.

prerequisites: ART 2300
description: Fundamentals of painting using acrylic and oil media applied to observation-based painting. Subject matter may include working from actual or two-dimensional references of still life, landscape, and the human form.

prerequisites: ART 2300 ; Membership in the University Honors Program
description: Fundamentals of painting using acrylic and oil media applied to observation-based painting. Subject matter may include working from actual or two-dimensional references of still life, landscape, and the human form.

prerequisites: Sophomore enrollment in BA in Art, BA in Art History, or BFA in Art
description: Introduction to the arts on campus and within the Ames community. Participation in gallery visits, art-related tours, presentations, and discussions to guide students in navigating and succeeding in our program. Offered on a satisfactory-fail basis only.

title_name: Collage, Assemblage, and the Found Object
description: Explores the significance of recycling, sustainable methods of art making, and thrift-store culture as medium and subject matter for artists in both 2D and 3D studio projects. Through selected readings, slide presentations, and studio activities, we will address the environmental cause and effect of materials and methods, consumption and waste, and personal responsibility as stewards for the planet we share. Emphasis will be on conceptual development.

title_name: Computer Modeling, Rendering and Virtual Photography
description: Introduction to 3D modeling using computer and available software. Modeling, texturing, lighting, and rendering with respect to 3D object and still scene creation.

title_name: Computer Modeling, Rendering and Virtual Photography: Honors
prerequisites: Membership in the University Honors Program
description: Introduction to 3D modeling using computer and available software. Modeling, texturing, lighting, and rendering with respect to 3D object and still scene creation.

title_name: Sources and Methods of Visual Art
description: Study and application of methods used by contemporary artists for the purpose of generating ideas for new work. Field trip.

Exploration of issues and directions in current art. Readings, discussions, and studio research projects to build an experimental and applied knowledge base for understanding each student's place in the contemporary art world.

prerequisites: ART 2020 or INDD 2010 or ARCH 3450 or Permission of Instructor
description: Design and production of basic functional and creative forms in wood. Develop a deeper understanding of the material through exploration of traditional and contemporary methods.

prerequisites: ART 2020 or INDD 2010 or Permission of Instructor; Membership in the University Honors Program
description: Design and production of basic functional and creative forms in wood. Develop a deeper understanding of the material through exploration of traditional and contemporary methods.

prerequisites: ART 2040 or permission of instructor
description: Investigation of expressive forms and techniques in ceramics; introduction to throwing on the wheel, to exploration of utilitarian and sculptural approaches in the medium, and to glaze-making research and electric kiln firing.

prerequisites: ART 2040 or Permission of Instructor; Membership in the University Honors Program
description: Investigation of expressive forms and techniques in ceramics; introduction to throwing on the wheel, to exploration of utilitarian and sculptural approaches in the medium, and to glaze-making research and electric kiln firing.

prerequisites: Art and Scientific Illustration Visualization Majors AND DSNS 1310 ; ART 2300 ; BIOL 2110 or BIOL 2120 or Permission of Instructor
description: Studio basics and professional techniques in black & white, continuous tone, and color. Introduction to professional practice and principles of communicating science through art. Emphasis on tools, materials, and rendering.

prerequisites: ART 2030 or Permission of Instructor
description: Hands-on studio experience. Focusing on traditional and contemporary metalsmithing techniques applicable to jewelry, functional objects, and sculptural art forms. Topics include fabrication, forming and cold joinery, with an emphasis on design and progressive skill development. Basic stone setting and lost wax casting introduced.

prerequisites: ART 2030 or Permission of Instructor; Membership in the University Honors Program
description: Hands-on studio experience. Focusing on traditional and contemporary metalsmithing techniques applicable to jewelry, functional objects, and sculptural art forms. Topics include fabrication, forming and cold joinery, with an emphasis on design and progressive skill development. Basic stone setting and lost wax casting introduced.
Contemporary issues in studio arts explored through lectures, presentations and critiques.

prerequisites: ART 3230 / SCIVZ 3230 , or permission of the instructor
description: An introduction to digital illustration software. Application of painting, drawing, and image-making techniques using vector and raster-based programs.

prerequisites: ART 3230 / SCIVZ 3230 , or permission of the instructor
description: Investigation of illustration as a form of communication. Emphasis on problem solving, effective composition, and advancement of rendering skills.

prerequisites: ART 2100 or permission of instructor
description: Continuation and expansion of concepts and processes from introductory photography. The class begins with advanced film camera techniques and experimentation with medium format cameras. It then moves into digital and color photography while also addressing output and presentation. Emphasis will be on the use of photography for visual communication and creative expression.

prerequisites: ART 2100 or Permission of Instructor; Membership in the University Honors Program
description: Continuation and expansion of concepts and processes from introductory photography. The class begins with advanced film camera techniques and experimentation with medium format cameras. It then moves into digital and color photography while also addressing output and presentation. Emphasis will be on the use of photography for visual communication and creative expression.

prerequisites: ART 2300
description: Drawing from the human figure.

prerequisites: ART 2300 ; Membership in the University Honors Program
description: Drawing from the human figure.

prerequisites: 6 credits ART courses or permission of instructor
description: Exploration of alternative materials (primarily non-metallics, both natural and manufactured) applicable to the design and creation of small designed objects and adornment. Students will learn additive and reductive processes, experiment with found object inclusion, rubber mold- making, and resin casting. A series of finishedpieces will result. Open to all majors in the College of Design.

prerequisites: ART 3270 / SCIVZ 3270
description: Rendering techniques applied to different types of biological and scientific subjects emphasizing communication. The use of traditional and digital media. Term project required.

prerequisites: ART 2380 or ART 2130
description: Painting using acrylic and oil media; composition and expression.

prerequisites: ART 2380 or ART 2130 ; Membership in the University Honors Program
description: Painting using acrylic and oil media; composition and expression.

title_name: Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Art and Design
description: Artificial intelligence tools for art and design: strategies, principles, and tools for AI in visual art, applied art, and creative industry practices. The aesthetics of AI systems in creative practices. An overview of working with AI as artistic and visual communication media including using AI and machine learning applications, histories of AI in the arts, generative deep learning systems, supervised and unsupervised machine learning tasks, and integrating AI systems through art and design practices. Producing AI multimedia outputs that include a range of forms from imagery to sound.
Introduction to woven textile construction with emphasis on technical development of weaving as a means for creative expression. Students may explore tapestry, off-loom and/or 4-harness weaving.

prerequisites: Membership in the University Honors Program
description: Introduction to woven textile construction with emphasis on technical development of weaving as a means for creative expression. Students may explore tapestry, off-loom and/or 4-harness weaving.

Introduction to natural dyes. Course includes a historical overview of natural dyes with attention to global perspectives. Emphasis on technical skill development and application of research in the creation of contemporary textile artwork.

prerequisites: Membership in the University Honors Program
description: Introduction to natural dyes. Course includes a historical overview of natural dyes with attention to global perspectives. Emphasis on technical skill development and application of research in the creation of contemporary textile artwork.

Fabric dyeing and printing techniques including block, stencil and experimental screen-printing, and resists using synthetic dyes, discharging agents, and pigments. Printing with plant-based dyes may also be explored. Digital printing and transfer printing on fabric may be introduced. Emphasis on research and development of dyed and printed textile design techniques as a means for creative expression.

prerequisites: Membership in the University Honors Program
description: Fabric dyeing and printing techniques including block, stencil, and experimental screen-printing, and resists using synthetic dyes, discharging agents, and pigments. Printing with plant-based dyes may also be explored. Digital printing and transfer printing on fabric may be introduced. Emphasis on research and development of dyed and printed textile design techniques as a means for creative expression.

prerequisites: Sophomore, Junior or Senior classification
description: Exploration of the potential of materials, form, and narrative in relation to letterpress printing and book arts. Traditional and contemporary artists' work will be used to discuss the history of printing, books, and paper. Emphasis will be placed on experimental and creative use of letterpress and book arts, with study of contemporary trends.

prerequisites: ART 2060 or instructor permission
description: In-depth exploration of digital or traditional design and block cutting processes (computer/laser cutter/CNC router or drawing/chisels) to produce black and white and color relief prints. Use relief printmaking to create a unified body of work. Use relief printmaking to create a unified body of prints from those blocks. Emphasis is on experimental and creative use of printmaking with study of contemporary trends.

prerequisites: ART 2060 and Credit or Enrollment in ART 2300 , or Instructor Permission; Membership in the University Honors Program
description: In-depth exploration of digital or traditional design and block cutting processes (computer/laser cutter/CNC router or drawing/chisels) to produce black and white and color relief prints. Use relief printmaking to create a unified body of work. Use relief printmaking to create a unified body of prints from those blocks. Emphasis is on experimental and creative use of printmaking with study of contemporary trends.

title_name: Intaglio and Monotype Printmaking: Digital / Traditional
prerequisites: ART 2060 and credit or enrollment in ART 2300 , or instructor permission
description: Explore the techniques and aesthetic qualities of black and white and color intaglio printmaking primarily through etching, aquatint, laser-cut plates and collagraph processes. Students will generate imagery through traditional drawing, collage and digital processes. Unique, one-of-a-kind black and white and color prints from Plexiglas will also be introduced. Emphasis is on experimental and creative use of printmaking for artistic expression.

title_name: Intaglio and Monotype Printmaking: Digital/Traditional: Honors
prerequisites: ART 2060 and Credit or Enrollment in ART 2300 , or Instructor Permission; Membership in the University Honors Program
description: Explore the techniques and aesthetic qualities of black and white and color intaglio printmaking primarily through etching, aquatint, laser-cut plates and collagraph processes. Students will generate imagery through traditional drawing, collage and digital processes. Unique, one-of-a-kind black and white and color prints from Plexiglas will also be introduced. Emphasis is on experimental and creative use of printmaking for artistic expression.

prerequisites: ART 2060 or instructor permission
description: Examine the techniques and aesthetic qualities of lithography primarily through stone lithography and hand-drawn and photographic plates. Students may generate imagery through traditional drawing, collage or digital processes. Emphasis is on experimental and creative use of printmaking for artistic expression. For those taking the course for a second semester, students can choose whether to focus on stone or plate lithography and there will be increased work with color.

prerequisites: ART 2060 and Credit or Enrollment in ART 2300 , or Instructor Permission; Membership in the University Honors Program
description: Examine the techniques and aesthetic qualities of lithography primarily through stone lithography and hand-drawn and photographic plates. Students may generate imagery through traditional drawing, collage or digital processes. Emphasis is on experimental and creative use of printmaking for artistic expression. For those taking the course for a second semester, students can choose whether to focus on stone or plate lithography and there will be increased work with color.

Overview of the following processes in screen printing: photo emulsion, stencils, rubylith, drawing fluid, screen print monotypes. Students generate imagery through traditional drawing, collage, and digital processes. Emphasis will be placed on experimental and creative use of printmaking, with study of contemporary trends.

Introduction to Chinese silk painting, an art form practiced for over 2,000 years. Gain a comprehensive understanding of silk painting’s history, techniques, and materials. Open to all skill levels. Artistic expression is encouraged through hands-on experience, blending traditional techniques and histories with innovative, contemporary interpretations.

Introduction to professional practices including development of portfolio (visual and written components). Lecture and presentation topics include applying to graduate school, internships, applying for jobs, grants/funding opportunities, professional networking, exhibition opportunities, and best practices for studio artists. Half-semester course. Required of all ISA majors.

title_name: Introduction to 3D Organic Modeling in ZBrush
description: Introduction to organic 3D model creation in ZBrush. Digital clay sculpting informed by traditional techniques. 3D printing pipeline. Emphasis on science research and visual design thinking for science communication. Some experience with other 3D modeling software beneficial, but not required.

prerequisites: ART 3080
description: Animation techniques using the computer and available software. Principles of character animation. Prior knowledge of modeling, lighting, texturing and rendering with available software is assumed.

prerequisites: ART 3080 ; Membership in the University Honors Program
description: Animation techniques using the computer and available software. Principles of character animation. Prior knowledge of modeling, lighting, texturing and rendering with available software is assumed.

prerequisites: ART 3080

prerequisites: ART 3080 ; Membership in the University Honors Program
description: Animation techniques using the computer and available software. Principles of animation. Prior knowledge of modeling, lighting, texturing, animation and rendering with computer and available software is assumed.

Independent project based creation and development of 'frivolous and non-frivolous' computer games in a cross-disciplinary team. Projects require cross-disciplinary teams. Aspects of Indie development and computer/video game history will be discussed.

title_name: Computer/Video Game Design and Development: Honors
prerequisites: Membership in the University Honors Program
description: Independent project based creation and development of 'frivolous and non-frivolous' computer games in a cross-disciplinary team. Projects require cross-disciplinary teams. Aspects of Indie development and computer/video game history will be discussed.

prerequisites: ART 3200 or Permission of Instructor
description: Advanced individual design and production of increasingly complex constructions, emphasizing development of a personal approach through research and exploration of materials, function, and specialized techniques.

prerequisites: ART 3200 ; Membership in the University Honors Program
description: Design and creation of increasingly complex furniture forms with consideration of precedents and innovative techniques and approaches. Continued development of a unique personal approach to the design and making of furniture. Refined sensitivity to wood, and continued consideration of various sustainable practices.

prerequisites: ART 3220
description: Creation of a body of work in personal ceramic forms and unique surface treatments. Exploration of kiln firings, research into contemporary ceramic artists and development of a body of increasingly skilled work are emphasized. Students are expected to be capable of independent studio work and take responsibility for firings.

prerequisites: ART 3220 ; Membership in the University Honors Program
description: Creation of a body of work in personal ceramic forms and unique surface treatments. Students are expected to be capable of independent studio work and take responsibility for firings. Honors project developed with supervision from instructor.

Emphasis on research and development of dyed and printed textile design techniques as a means for creative expression.

prerequisites: Junior classification in College of Design or junior classification in FDM
description: This hands-on studio course will allow students to explore digital printing technology and its application to textile design for those working within industry as well as independent studio practitioners. Digital design development includes pattern repeats and photo manipulation to create unique textile designs for fashion, interior and fine art applications.

description: Independent, advanced work in printmaking processes. Emphasis is on development of a unified body of work and research into contemporary artists.

title_name: Sustainable Design and Fabrication of Furniture
prerequisites: Senior or Graduate classification or Permission of Instructor
description: Issues in the design and fabrication of furniture focusing on sustainable practices. An exploration of current practices and their environmental effects, consumer demands, and alternative processes and materials.
Introduction to aspects of community arts management and art gallery operations. Students will assist in the conception, design, and realization of exhibitions.

prerequisites: Senior or Graduate classification or Permission of Instructor
description: The artist/designer's role in sustainable development with a focus on cultural understanding of the collaborating communities. Class discussion, visual exercises, and the creation of creative collaborative service-learning projects such as product design, habitat design, and visual arts projects. Preorientation for travel to Ghana in ARTIS 4660/5660X. Meets International Perspectives Requirement.

prerequisites: ART 4650 or Permission of Instructor
description: Traveling studio to Africa; an experiential tour of arts and history combined with design-focused collaborative service-learning projects. Projects may include product development, design consultation, sustainable building design, and learning and teaching of visual arts. Student teams will develop projects in partnership with in-country nationals. Meets International Perspectives Requirement.

Data visualization strategies, principles, and tools for encoding data in visual art, applied art, and creative industry practices. The aesthetics of encoded data in creative practices. An overview of working with data as artistic and visual communication media including data acquisition, organization, representation, and interaction with data through art and design. Producing data display outputs that include a range of displays from screen to installation.

Usage of professional video editing software and application of best practices for video production and post-production to realize original artworks. Creation of narrative and non-narrative videos and site specific video installations. Prominent examples in the history of video art provide context for the coursework. Non-repeatable for graduate students.

Create software and integrate the sensors required to create interactive artworks, video games, and installations. Prominent examples in the history of interactive art provide context for the coursework.

description: Figurative and non-figurative painting with advanced work in media, composition, and theory.

title_name: Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Art and Design
description: Artificial intelligence tools for art and design: strategies, principles, and tools for AI in visual art, applied art, and creative industry practices. The aesthetics of AI systems in creative practices. An overview of working with AI as artistic and visual communication media including using AI and machine learning applications, histories of AI in the arts, generative deep learning systems, supervised and unsupervised machine learning tasks, and integrating AI systems through art and design practices. Producing AI multimedia outputs that include a range of forms from imagery to sound.

prerequisites: Graduate Standing or Permission of Instructor
description: Fabric dyeing and printing techniques including block, stencil and experimental screen-printing, and resists using synthetic dyes, discharging agents, and pigments. Printing with plant-based dyes may also be explored. Digital printing and transfer printing on fabric may be introduced. Emphasis on research and development of dyed and printed textile design techniques as a means for creative expression.

prerequisites: Graduate Standing or Permission of Instructor
description: This hands-on studio course will allow students to explore digital printing technology and its application to textile design for those working within industry as well as independent studio practitioners. Digital design development includes pattern repeats and photo manipulation to create unique textile designs for fashion, interior and fine art applications.

prerequisites: Graduate Standing or Permission of Instructor
description: Students will explore the potential of materials, form, and narrative in relation to letterpress printing and book arts. Traditional and contemporary artists' work will be used to discuss the history of printing, books, and paper. Emphasis will be placed on experimental and creative use of letterpress and book arts, with study of contemporary trends.

prerequisites: Graduate Classification or Permission of Instructor
description: In-depth exploration of digital or traditional design and block cutting processes (computer/laser cutter/CNC router or drawing/chisels) to produce black and white and color relief prints. Use relief printmaking to create a unified body of work. Use relief printmaking to create a unified body of prints from those blocks. Emphasis is on experimental and creative use of printmaking with study of contemporary trends.

title_name: Intaglio and Monotype Printmaking: Digital / Traditional
prerequisites: Graduate Standing or Permission of Instructor
description: Explore the techniques and aesthetic qualities of black and white and color intaglio printmaking primarily through etching, aquatint, laser-cut plates and collagraph processes. Students will generate imagery through traditional drawing, collage and digital processes. Unique, one-of-a-kind black and white and color prints from Plexiglas will also be introduced. Emphasis is on experimental and creative use of printmaking for artistic expression.

prerequisites: Graduate Classification or Permission of Instructor
description: Examine the techniques and aesthetic qualities of lithography primarily through stone lithography and hand-drawn and photographic plates. Students may generate imagery through traditional drawing, collage or digital processes. Emphasis is on experimental and creative use of printmaking for artistic expression. For those taking the course for a second semester, students can choose whether to focus on stone or plate lithography and there will be increased work with color.

prerequisites: Graduate Classification or Permission of Instructor
description: Exploration of the potential of materials, form, and different techniques related to screen printing, including, but not limited to: photo emulsion, stencils, rubylith, drawing fluid, screen filler, and screen print monotypes. Traditional and contemporary artists' work will be used to discuss the history of screen printing. Imagery will be created through traditional drawing, collage, photography, as well as digital processes. Emphasis will be placed on experimental and creative use of printmaking, with study of contemporary trends.

prerequisites: Graduate Standing or Permission of Instructor
description: Introduction to Chinese silk painting, an art form practiced for over 2,000 years. Gain a comprehensive understanding of silk painting’s history, techniques, and materials. Open to all skill levels. Artistic expression is encouraged through hands-on experience, blending traditional techniques and histories with innovative, contemporary interpretations.

prerequisites: Graduate Standing or Permission of Instructor
description: Data visualization: strategies, principles, and tools for encoding data in visual art, applied art, and creative industry practices. The aesthetics of encoded data in creative practices. An overview of working with data as artistic and visual communication media including data acquisition, organization, representing, and interacting with data through art and design. Producing data display outputs that include a range of displays from screen to installation.

prerequisites: Graduate enrollment in College of Design
description: Through directed readings, individual assignments, guest presentations, local field trips, and group discussions, students learn strategies to create and apply a personalized plan that will guide them toward successful studio practice at all stages of their careers. Topics include professional opportunities and avenues of support available to studio artists, development in preparing visual portfolios, artists statements, and applications for grants, residencies, and exhibitions.

title_name: Graduate Seminar: Critique and Creative Process
prerequisites: Graduate enrollment in College of Design
description: Ongoing weekly critiques and dialog about sources, methods, and progress of studio projects. Graduate students will learn to articulate their ideas from concept to creation. The interaction of students at different levels in a broad spectrum of studio courses will reveal commonalities and connections among the visual arts, accelerating individual creative development.

prerequisites: Graduate standing
description: Usage of professional video editing software and application of best practices for video production and post-production to realize original artworks. Creation of narrative and non-narrative videos and site specific video installations. prominent examples in the history of video art provide context for the coursework. Non-repeatable for graduate students.

prerequisites: Graduate standing
description: Create software and integrate the sensors required to create interactive artworks, video games, and installations. Prominent examples in the history of interactive art provide context for the coursework.
Special issues related to studio art. Topics vary each time offered.

prerequisites: Graduate classification, ART 4940 or permission of instructor
description: International study abroad program in western Europe. Visits to design studios, art museums, and educational facilities. Related activities depending on specific area of study which may vary each time offered. Meets International Perspectives Requirement.
Research strategies related to fine art and technology. Application of selected methods to specific issues.
Exploration and application of media with various materials, methods and ideas.

prerequisites: Graduate classification and written approval of supervising instructor on required form in advance of semester of enrollment
